arts.ttl: Enzo Maresca continues to play down Chelsea’s title chances despite latest win
arts.sum: The Blues moved to within two points of Premier League leaders Liverpool with Sunday’s victory over Brentford.

arts.ttl: Brentford very close to 'deserved' draw - Frank
arts.sum: Boss Thomas Frank praises Brentford's performance and says a draw with Chelsea would have been "well deserved" as they lose 2-1 at Stamford Bridge.

arts.ttl: Marseca explains why Chelsea are still ‘not ready’ and gives updates on Felix and Lavia injuries
arts.sum: Chelsea reaction. The post Marseca explains why Chelsea are still ‘not ready’ and gives updates on Felix and Lavia injuries appeared first on West London Sport.

arts.ttl: Marc Cucurella masters ‘three positions’ to help Chelsea retain title charge
arts.sum: Chelsea 2-1 Brentford: The Spaniard’s all-action display helped the Blues close the gap on Liverpool to just two points at the top of the Premier League, though manager Enzo Maresca maintains his side are not in the title race

arts.ttl: Chelsea showed spirit but not ready to compete for title - Maresca
arts.sum: Head coach Enzo Maresca praises Chelsea's spirit but says they are "not ready to compete" for the Premier League title as they close in on leaders Liverpool following their 2-1 win against Brentford.

arts.ttl: Liverpool fans hammer Fabio Carvalho for what he did during Chelsea 2-1 Brentford
Enzo Maresca explains why Chelsea are "not ready" to win Premier League title
arts.sum: Marc Cucurella set Chelsea on their way in 2-1 win over Brentford only to be shown a red card after the final whistle was blown after appearing to push Kevin Schade

arts.ttl: Man City set for '£200m January transfer boost' as Arsenal and Chelsea watch on
arts.sum: Despite these hurdles, the Mirror reports that manager Pep Guardiola, who reaffirmed his commitment to the club by signing a fresh two-year contract last month, will be empowered with a substantial transfer budget come January. Arsenal and Chelsea will be on alert as Premier League title rival Manchester City are reportedly set to receive a £200m kitty in the January transfer window as the club aims to tackle a string of disappointing results and pivot their season back on track.

arts.ttl: Why Marc Cucurella was sent off for Chelsea as Premier League release statement after bust-up
arts.sum: Marc Cucurella received a second yellow card for Chelsea after the full-time whistle due to his post-match actions against Brentford. Cucurella will now miss Chelsea's next Premier League game, which is away to Everton.

arts.ttl: Chelsea player ratings as Cucurella scores, Caicedo masterful, Jackson redemption vs Brentford - Bobby Vincent - football.london
arts.sum: Marc Cucurella gave the Blues the lead not long before half-time with a wonderful header after being found by Noni Madueke's cross from the right. Robert Sanchez - Quiet night for the 'keeper until 73 minutes when he was called into action to deny a goal-bound volley from Norgaard.
